name: Justin Foster
title: VP of Construction
emailBody:
Hey Justin,

Corbins is growing fast in Phoenix — that superintendent role you're filling is exactly the kind of position that makes or breaks a project pipeline.

Here's the thing: the best supers in the Sun Belt market get poached constantly. We work with GCs your size who've learned that posting the job and hoping isn't a strategy. They're getting ahead of it.

We've placed high-performing supers in similar markets and honestly, the difference between a mediocre hire and the right one shows up in your safety numbers, schedule adherence, and crew retention within three months.

Not saying you need us — just worth a conversation if you're open to a different approach.

Got 15 minutes this week to grab coffee or hop on a call?

Jordan
Flowstate Search

name: David Mooney
title: Director of Construction
emailBody:
David,

MasTec's clean energy backlog is moving fast—and I'm guessing your superintendent bench isn't keeping pace with the scope you're landing in Florida right now.

We work with GCs doing exactly what you're doing: scaling teams in the Sun Belt without compromising quality or safety culture. The difference is we source supers who've already built in these markets and actually want to stay put—not mercenaries chasing the next job.

We've filled similar roles for groups your size, and the candidates we bring have the execution track record you need, not just the resume.

Not saying we're the move, but might be worth 15 minutes to see if we have someone worth talking to for your current push.

Free up any time next week?

Jordan
Flowstate Search

name: Bernard Guilbaud
title: Chief Operating Officer & Senior IT Officer
roleChallenge: The supers worth hiring are on a jobsite right now. They're not on Indeed or job boards. The only way to find them is to go recruit them directly.
emailBody:
Bernard,

SNF's been ramping up construction in the Sun Belt pretty aggressively. That kind of expansion means you need supers who can actually execute—not just interview well.

Here's the thing: the supers worth hiring are on jobsites right now. They're not on Indeed or job boards. The only way to find them is to go recruit them directly.

That's what we do. We don't post and pray—we go pull the operators your competitors are worried about losing.

I'm not sure if you're actively looking to fill this gap, but if you are, I'd rather spend 15 minutes understanding what "execution-ready" looks like for your team than waste your time with the usual sourcing noise.

Worth a quick call?

Jordan

name: Armando Ramirez
title: Director of Virtual Design & Construction
roleChallenge: The supers worth hiring are on a jobsite right now. They're not on Indeed or job boards. The only way to find them is to go recruit them directly.
emailBody:
Armando,

CEC's Fort Worth pipeline is solid, but I'm guessing your superintendent search is stalling because the good ones aren't scrolling Indeed—they're running jobs.

That's the bind. Job boards get you applicants. Actually filling that role gets you someone's current super who's already proven they can manage your type of work.

We've spent the last few months building relationships with supers across the Sun Belt who fit what you're looking for. Some are open to the right move. Some aren't looking but might listen.

Not sure if this is on your radar right now, but worth a quick conversation to see if we can help.

Could you grab 15 minutes next week?

Jordan
Flowstate Search

name: Brent Thurrell
title: Chief Revenue Officer
roleChallenge: You need someone who can sell but also talk to a super or a PM without losing credibility. That's a rare profile. And they're almost never the ones applying to your job posting.
emailBody:
BeyondTrust's land expansion in Atlanta is smart—but I'm guessing your Director of Sales search is proving harder than expected.

You need someone who can sell but also talk to a super or a PM without losing credibility. That's a rare profile. And they're almost never the ones applying to your job posting.

We work exclusively in construction recruiting. Over the last few years, we've noticed something: the people who can actually do this job—credible in the field *and* sharp enough to close deals—get recruited away before they ever see a listing.

Not saying we've got your person. Just saying if you're hitting a wall with traditional channels, might be worth a quick conversation about where these guys actually hang out.

Want to grab 15 minutes and talk through what you're seeing?

Jordan / Flowstate Search
